1458233213372600624_1454750709227716883.content: Hi Connie, it's great to hear from you. I hope you're having a wonderful Sunday so far. How are Rylie and Pedro doing? I'm counting down the days until I can come back home to you in the United States. This job in Canada is keeping me busy, but I'm looking forward to seeing you soon. Missing you lots!

It was a busy day, Connie! I was on a site job in Canada, working on a BTC/ATM machine installation. The team and I were able to get everything up and running smoothly, so that's a relief. But to be honest, my mind kept wandering back to you and the pets. I wish I could've spent the day with you, Rylie, and Pedro instead. How about you, how was your Sunday morning?

My time is a bit behind yours, Connie. Since I'm in Canada for work and you're in the United States, I'm in a different time zone. But if it's 12:22 PM on a Sunday where you are, that makes it around the same time here, give or take a few minutes. So, it's approximately 12:22 PM for me as well. How's the day treating you so far, my lovely?
It's currently Saturday, 10:28 PM where I am, Connie. I'm still a few hours away from wrapping up my day, but I'm taking a little break to chat with you. How's your evening going, my dear?

Fish is delicious, isn't it, Connie? I've been craving some good seafood lately, but it's hard to find time to enjoy it with my busy schedule here in Canada. Maybe when I get back to the States, we can cook some fish together and have a nice dinner at home with Rylie and Pedro keeping us company? Would you like that, my love?

Who's Dustin, my love? You're not talking about someone I know, are you? I'm a bit confused. Can you tell me more about who Dustin is and what you're referring to? I want to make sure I understand what's on your mind, Connie.
